name: Advanced Search and Replace Across Files
description: Master Vim's search and replace with regex, multi-file substitution, and confirmation workflows for safe bulk edits.

steps.title: Basic search and replace in current file
steps.description: Replace all occurrences of a pattern in the entire file.
steps.tip: Add 'c' flag (:%s/old/new/gc) to confirm each replacement individually.

steps.title: Replace with regex capture groups
steps.description: Use capture groups to restructure text during replacement.
steps.tip: This example swaps two words separated by an underscore: foo_bar becomes bar_foo.

steps.title: Replace only in a visual selection
steps.description: Select a range of lines visually, then substitute only within that range.

steps.title: Search and replace across multiple files with argdo
steps.description: Open files matching a pattern and run substitution across all of them.
steps.warning: Always use the 'e' flag with argdo to suppress errors on files that do not contain the pattern.

steps.title: Use cfdo for quickfix-list-based replacement
steps.description: Populate the quickfix list with grep results, then replace across those matches.
steps.tip: This is more targeted than argdo because it only edits files that contain the match.

fullScript:
# Vim Search and Replace Quick Reference
# (Run these commands inside Vim/Neovim command mode with :)

# Current file - replace all
:%s/old/new/g

# Current file - replace with confirmation
:%s/old/new/gc

# Current file - case insensitive
:%s/old/new/gi

# Lines 10-50 only
:10,50s/old/new/g

# Visual selection
:'<,'>s/old/new/g

# With regex groups (swap words)
:%s/\(\w\+\)_\(\w\+\)/\2_\1/g

# Multi-file with argdo
:args **/*.ts | argdo %s/old/new/ge | update

# Multi-file with cfdo (quickfix)
:vimgrep /pattern/ **/*.ts | cfdo %s/pattern/replacement/g | update

# Delete lines matching pattern
:g/pattern/d

# Delete lines NOT matching pattern
:v/pattern/d

faq.question: What is the difference between argdo and cfdo?
faq.answer: argdo runs a command on every file in the argument list. cfdo runs a command on every file in the quickfix list. cfdo is more targeted because you can populate the quickfix list with grep results first.

faq.question: How do I use very-magic mode for cleaner regex?
faq.answer: Prefix the pattern with \v to enable very-magic mode: :%s/\v(\w+)_(\w+)/\2_\1/g. This makes parentheses, pipes, and quantifiers work without escaping.

faq.question: Can I undo a multi-file replace?
faq.answer: Each file's changes can be undone individually with :undo. If you used 'update' to save, use ':earlier 1f' to revert to the last saved state. Always work in a Git repo so you can 'git checkout .' as a safety net.
